subject: biology
text: Which of the following conditions is NOT necessary for photosynthesis to take place?

subject: biology
text: The insects in which the maxillae are modified into a long coiled proboscis is

subject: biology
text: When a cell is placed in solution and the size of the cell increases, the concentration of solution is said to be

subject: biology
text: Which of the following plant hormone is responsible for ripening of fruits?

subject: biology
text: The ability of a living organism to detect and respond to changes in the environment is referred to as

subject: biology
text: An example of an endospermous seed is

subject: biology
text: Which of the following theories was NOT considered by Darwin in his evolutionary theory?

subject: biology
text: Which of the following protects the essential parts of the flower at bud stage?

subject: biology
text: which of the following is an agent of a sexually transmitted disease?
explanation: Treponema pallidum is the causative agent of syphilis, which is a sexually transmitted disease.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following animals has homodont dentition?
explanation: Homodont dentition refers to teeth that are all the same shape. Lizards generally have teeth that are similar in shape (homodont).

subject: biology
text: What is the primary function of cofactors?
explanation: Most enzymes do not function optimally, or even at all, unless bound to other non-protein helper molecules called cofactors.

subject: biology
text: Coloration of the eye is ______ variation
explanation: Eye coloration is a caused by morphological variation

subject: biology
text: Which of the following choices correctly describes the composition of the cell membrane?
explanation: It consists of a lipid bi-layer with embedded proteins. The basic function of the cell membrane is to protect the cell from its surroundings.

subject: biology
text: The feeding relationship among a set of members of a community is referred to as _______?
explanation: Feeding relationships between organisms in a community are represented by food chains.

subject: biology
text: Nitrogen is released into the atmosphere when ________
explanation: Nitrogen is essential to all living systems. During electrical storms, large amounts of nitrogen are oxidized and united with water to produce an acid that falls to Earth in rainfall and deposits nitrates in the soil.

subject: biology
text: Size, colour and fingerprints are examples of ______
explanation: Morphological variation includes aspects of the outward appearance i.e shape, structure, color, pattern, size.

subject: biology
text: The level of organism in spirogyra and volvox is
explanation: spirogyra and volvox are cells

subject: biology
text: Which of the following scenarios is an example of cohesion?

options.A: Water molecules stick to the surface of a mirror.
options.B: Water sticks to the walls of blood vessels.
options.C: Water molecules stay on the tip of a pine needle because the water is attracted to the surface of the needle.
options.D: Water molecules stick to one another and form a bead

explanation: Cohesion is an ability of water molecule to stick to one another due to the presence of hydrogen bonding

subject: biology
text: The common thing in plant and animal cell is
explanation: Nucleus is a specialized structure occurring in most cells. The nucleus controls and regulates the activities of the cell.

subject: biology
text: A group of closely related organisms capable of interbreeding to produce the fertile offspring are known as members of a
explanation: An example of a species is all human beings.

subject: biology
text: A beaker of pond water containing few specimens of Euglena was placed in a dark room for two weeks. At the end of this period, the specimens of Euglena were still alive because they were

options.A: able to carry out holozoic nutrition
options.B: able to carry out photosynthesis using carbon dioxide in the pond water
options.C: better adapted to life in darkness than to life in light

explanation: Holozoic nutrition is a type of heterotrophic nutrition that is characterized by the ingestion and internal processing of gaseous, liquid or solid food particles.

subject: biology
text: The cytoplasm of a cell is considered a very important component because it

options.A: regulates the amount of energy in the cell
options.C: is the outermost part of the cell
options.D: is solely responsible for cell division

explanation: Most of the important activities of the cell occur in the cytoplasm. It helps to fill out the cell and keeps organelles in their place.

subject: biology
text: Red blood cells were found to have burst open after being placed in distill for an hour. This phenomenon is known as
explanation: Haemolysis is the rupture or destruction of red blood cells.

subject: biology
text: The curvature movement of plants in response to the stimulus of water is called

subject: biology
text: The longest bone in the body is the
explanation: Femur is your thighbone. It is the longest and strongest bone in your body.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is a behavioural adaptation that protects prey from predators?
explanation: Secreting irritating fluid is a behavioural adaptation that protects preys from predators.

subject: biology
text: The period of inactivity seen in some animals during long period of heat or drought is
explanation: Aestivation is a state of animal dormancy, similar to hibernation. It takes place during times of heat and dryness.

subject: biology
text: The insect trapping movements of the leaves of the venus fly trap is a behavioural adaptation for
explanation: Insect traping movement of leaves is a behavioural adaptation for obtaining food.

subject: biology
text: Animals are said to gregarious when

options.A: they survive adverse conditions of cold weather
options.B: they transmit information from one individual to another
options.C: they are of the same species moving together in a group
options.D: they give an alarm to alert others to show the normal protective behaviour of the group when danger threatens

explanation: Gregarious behaviour is commonly associated with social insects that live in communal colonies.

subject: biology
text: The type of nutrition shown by spirogyra is
explanation: Spirogyra undergoes holophytic nutrition. Spirogyra's mode of nutrition is photosynthesis.

subject: biology
text: Raw materials required by green plants to manufacture their food are
explanation: Chlorophyll, sunlight, and raw materials are needed by plants to manufacture their food.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following pairs of organisms are photosynthetic?
explanation: Euglena and Chlamydomonas are photosynthetic. They both possess features of plants which enables them to produce their food.

subject: biology
text: The type of nutrition in which two organisms of different species live together and derive nutrients from each other is
explanation: Symbiotic nutrition is the relationship between the organisms in which they share their nutrition and shelter.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following statement about the similarities in plant and animal transport is correct?

options.A: Materials and hormones are transported in fluid form
options.B: The transport medium is made up of only tissues
options.C: Their materials are transported in the same vessels

explanation: Both animals and plants have more than one type of tissue which is specialised to make transport an efficient process.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following describes the function of lymph
explanation: Lymph help rid the body of toxins, waste and other unwanted materials.

subject: biology
text: The hormone which regulates the amount of glucose in the blood us called?
explanation: Insulin is a hormone that lowers the level of glucose in the blood. It's made by the beta cells of the pancreas.

subject: biology
text: Deamination occurs in the?
explanation: Deamination, the process of removing an amino group from a molecule, primarily occurs in the liver.

subject: biology
text: The hypha of rhizopus is said to be non-septate because it
explanation: The hyphae of Rhizopus are described as non-septate because they lack cross-walls (septa).

subject: biology
text: The function of ribosome in cells is
explanation: The function of a ribosome in a cell is protein synthesis. Ribosomes are the sites where mRNA is translated into proteins.

subject: biology
text: In which of the following does external fertilization take place?
explanation: External fertilization is common in aquatic environments. Toads are amphibians, and amphibians typically exhibit external fertilization in water.

subject: biology
text: The villus in the small intestine is significant because

options.A: Increases the surface area for absorption
options.B: increases the surface area for digestion

explanation: The villi are small, finger-like projections that line the inner surface of the small intestine. Their primary function is to increase the surface area available for the absorption of digested nutrients into the bloodstream.

subject: biology
text: The butterfly is of great economic importance because

options.A: of it's use in scientific studies
options.C: it adds to the beauty of the environment
options.D: it pollinates flowers of crops and other plants

explanation: Butterflies play important roles as pollinators to most agricultural crops.

subject: biology
text: The bright colours of the comb and feathers in the peacock are for?
explanation: The male peacocks display their beautiful fanned trains as part of their courtship behaviour.

subject: biology
text: In an agricultural ecosystem, the biotic component consist of
explanation: Biotic components are any living component that affects another organism or shapes the ecosystem.

subject: biology
text: Epiphytes growing on the branches of trees provide an example of the relationship known as?
explanation: The relationship between epiphytes and their host trees is an example of commensalism, where one organism benefits and the other is neither harmed nor helped.

subject: biology
text: One of the functions of the xylem is?
explanation: xylem, plant vascular tissue that conveys water and dissolved minerals from the roots to the rest of the plant and also provides physical support.

subject: biology
text: Which of the statements below is not characteristic of wind-pollinated flowers?

options.A: Stigmas are usually large and feathery
options.C: The pollen grains have rough spiny surfaces

explanation: Wind-pollinated flowers have pollen that is smooth, light and easily airborne, not rough and spiny.

subject: biology
text: Some of the features of an animal are scales, teeth, nares, and backbone. The animal is likely to be a?
explanation: Reptiles such as the lizard have a dry skin with scales, teeth, and a backbone.

subject: biology
text: Choose the sequence which represents the correct order of organisms in a food chain
explanation: The functional group in a food chain include: Primary producer to Primary consumer to Secondary consumer: Grass, grasshopper, toad, snake, hawk.

subject: biology
text: Flatworms and roundworms are said to be invertebrates because?

options.B: they can live inside the vertebrates

explanation: Invertebrates are animals that neither possess nor develop a vertebral column, derived from the notochord.

subject: biology
text: When a Spirogyra cell is immersed in a salt solution more concentrated than its cell sap, it?
explanation: Exosmosis is the movement of water outside the cells when a cell is placed in a hypertonic solution. The cell becomes flaccid.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is NOT regarded as a pollutant on land or in the air?
explanation: Nitrogen is a colourless, odourless, tasteless gas that is the most plentiful element in Earth's atmosphere and is a constituent of all living matter; it is not a pollutant.

subject: biology
text: The initial volume of water poured into a bag of dry soil was 50ml and the amount that drained through was 35mI. The percentage water content of the fully soaked soil is therefore?
explanation: Percentage of water content retained = (50 - 35)/50 * 100 = 30%

subject: biology
text: When the original king and queen of termites die, they are replaced by

options.A: the king and queen of another colony
options.B: some adult reproductives from the same colony
options.C: some adult workers which are specially fed to breed
options.D: developing nymphs nurtured as secondary reproductives

explanation: When the queen dies, secondary reproductives may be produced from developing nymphs.

subject: biology
text: In demonstrating the importance of mineral elements in plants, the culture bottle must be darkened to?

options.A: prevent algal growth in culture solution

explanation: Always use a completely opaque water storage tank - Algae uses photosynthesis to reproduce.

subject: biology
text: sources of air pollutants are?

options.A: industrial chimneys, burning fossil oils and river dams
options.C: sulphur dioxide, vehicle exhausts, and aerosols

explanation: Common sources of air pollution include household combustion devices, motor vehicles, industrial facilities, and forest fires.

subject: biology
text: The body of a snail is divided into head?
explanation: The main parts of a snail's body include the head, the visceral hump, and the foot.

subject: biology
text: An adaptation for defense in animals is?
explanation: The body of the porcupine fish is covered in spines used as a defence mechanism.

subject: biology
text: The movement of Euglena towards the source of light is a?
explanation: This change in direction of movement in response to an external stimulus is called taxis and is a tactic movement.

subject: biology
text: During mitosis, the stage at which chromosomes lines up around the equator is?
explanation: The stages of mitosis are prophase, prometaphase, metaphase, anaphase, and telophase. During metaphase chromosomes line up at the equator.

subject: biology
text: The waste product of insects is?
explanation: The primary nitrogenous waste product produced by the majority of terrestrial insects is uric acid.

subject: biology
text: Succession that occurs on an abandoned farm land is?

subject: biology
text: In Nigeria, Southern Guinea Savanna is found in?
explanation: The Southern Guinea Savanna vegetation zone in Nigeria covers states like Kogi and Kwara in the central region.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is associated with the dark stage of photosynthesis?
explanation: The dark stage converts carbon dioxide and other compounds into sugars without requiring light.

subject: biology
text: The part of mammalian skin that excretes metabolic wastes is?
explanation: The body removes waste by secreting water, sodium salts, and nitrogenous waste onto the skin surface using sweat glands.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is the most inclusive level of classification in the Linnaean system?
explanation: The Linnaean system from most inclusive to least inclusive goes Domain, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is a method of asexual reproduction in plants?
explanation: Vegetative propagation produces new individuals from the vegetative parts of a single parent plant, such as stems, roots, or leaves, without seeds or spores.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following is the primary organ involved in gas exchange during respiration in humans?
explanation: The lungs contain tiny air sacs called alveoli where the exchange of oxygen and carbon dioxide occurs.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following statements about the heart is true?

options.A: The heart is responsible for the production of red blood cells
options.B: The heart pumps oxygenated blood to the lungs
options.C: The heart receives blood from the kidneys and filters waste products
options.D: The heart is a muscular organ that contracts to circulate blood throughout the body

explanation: The heart is a muscular organ that contracts to circulate blood throughout the body.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following characteristics is typical of the phylum Arthropoda?
explanation: Arthropods are characterized by a segmented body divided into segments such as head, thorax, abdomen.

subject: biology
text: Which process in the nutrient cycle converts atmospheric nitrogen into a form that plants can utilize?
explanation: Nitrogen fixation, typically done by nitrogen-fixing bacteria, converts atmospheric nitrogen gas into ammonia or related compounds that plants can use.

subject: biology
text: A biome characterized by hot summer, warm winter and treeless vegetation is
explanation: Steppe grasslands are regions with vast expanses of grasses and herbs but largely devoid of trees, with hot summers and relatively warm winters.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following describes the inheritance of traits from parents to offspring?
explanation: Genetics is the branch of biology that studies how traits are passed from parents to their offspring through genes.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following options correctly identifies excretory organs in animals?
explanation: Lungs, kidneys, and skin play a role in eliminating waste products from the body.

subject: biology
text: Which organ removes the largest quantity of water from the blood?
explanation: The kidneys are primarily responsible for regulating the water balance in the body, filtering blood and excreting excess water as urine.

subject: biology
text: The organisms that adopt swarming as an adaptation to overcome overcrowding are
explanation: When a termite colony becomes overcrowded, winged reproductive individuals swarm, leave the original colony, and establish new colonies.

subject: biology
text: Bryophyte is an intermediate group between higher algae and
explanation: Bryophytes are considered to be intermediate between higher algae and pteridophytes. Pteridophytes are the first vascular plants.

subject: biology
text: One main feature of trees in the savanna habitat is the possession of

options.B: Straight slender stems with branches near the top

explanation: Thick, corky bark protects the trees from damage caused by fire and helps conserve water during the dry season.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following structures enables the exchange of gases in insects?
explanation: The tracheae are a network of air-filled tubes that branch throughout the insect's body, allowing direct gas exchange between tissues and the environment.

subject: biology
text: A community with a population of two million three hundred and ten thousand people living in an area of two thousand three hundred and ten square kilometres has a population density of
explanation: Population density = Population / Area = 2,310,000 / 2,310 = 1000

subject: biology
text: Similar structures that are modified to work in different ways in different organisms are referred to as
explanation: Homologous structures are anatomical features that share a common ancestry but have evolved to serve different functions in different organisms.

subject: biology
text: Which of the following plants shows hypogeal germination?
explanation: In hypogeal germination the cotyledons remain underground. Maize exhibits hypogeal germination, while castor oil, groundnut, and orange show epigeal germination.

subject: biology
text: Bilateral symmetry, cylindrical bodies and double openings are characteristic features of
explanation: Nematodes (roundworms) have bilateral symmetry, cylindrical bodies, and a complete digestive system with mouth and anus.
